title: Sprain/strain (starter evidence summary)
text:
Sprains/strains are common after twisting injuries. Telephone triage focuses on fracture/dislocation and neurovascular compromise.

Emergency red flags (ER now):
- Numbness/weakness or the limb is cold/pale compared to the other side (possible neurovascular compromise)

Urgent evaluation (same-day):
- Obvious deformity or inability to bear weight/use the limb
- Open wound with significant bleeding
- Severe swelling or severe pain after injury

Supportive care (if mild and stable):
- Rest, ice, compression, elevation (RICE) if safe and appropriate.
- Consider in-person evaluation if pain persists, function worsens, or red flags appear.
